keyPoints: A 2:1 multiplexer is a basic digital circuit that selects one of three input signals and passes it to the output.
A 2:1 multiplexer is used to select one of three inputs (A, B, and C) and pass it to the output.
The select lines (S) control the selection process, with one bit controlling the selection of two inputs.
The 2:1 multiplexer has two inputs (A and B) and one output (Z).
The 2:1 multiplexer has three inputs (A, B, and C) and one output (Z).
A 4:1 multiplexer can be implemented using two 2:1 multiplexers, with the outputs of the two 2:1 multiplexers connected to the select lines of the next 2:1 multiplexer.
